title: gruAI Marketing Launch — Rename, README, Content Strategy

output.rationale: Multiple valid approaches (naming, content strategy, platform mix), crosses 3+ domains (engineering, content, marketing), lasting brand consequences.

output.summary: Read directive.md, vision.md, README.md, package.json. Package already published as 'gru-ai' on npm. GitHub repo still 'agent-conductor'. README exists but needs marketing polish.

Vision: autonomous AI company framework. Key differentiators: pixel-art office visualization, structured pipeline, C-suite personalities. Competitors: Devin ($500/mo headless), CrewAI (YAML configs). npm name is gru-ai, GitHub repo is agent-conductor. No preferences.md exists.

brainstorm.agent: C-suite (Priya CMO, Marcus CPO, Sarah CTO)

output.summary: Phase 1 proposals + Phase 2 deliberation complete. Convergence on: rename to gruai, lead with pixel-art GIF, static HTML landing page. Key disagreements: 小红书-first vs Reddit-first, full marketing strategy vs minimal viable launch, AI video vs real screencasts. Brand conflict flagged: Gru.ai (BabelCloud, $5.5M funded, pivoted to gbox.ai).

5 projects: repo-rename (P0, Devon), readme-overhaul (P0, Taylor), xiaohongshu-strategy (P1, Taylor), reddit-strategy (P1, Taylor), landing-page (P2, Riley). Sequential dependency chain: rename → README → content strategies + landing page.
Skipped — brainstorm phase already audited codebase (Sarah CTO confirmed: only 3 source files reference agent-conductor, no CI/CD, demo.gif exists). Technical risk is low.
All 5 projects decomposed into tasks: repo-rename (3 tasks), readme-overhaul (2 tasks), xiaohongshu-strategy (3 tasks), reddit-strategy (1 task), landing-page (2 tasks). Total: 11 tasks.
Using existing 'game' branch. No worktree needed.
